title: Picking up the tab at a restaurant
conversation: Tanya: Let me pick up the tab. John: No, it's my treat. The guy is supposed to pay on a date! Tanya: Says who? I don't want you to pay for me every time we go out! Let's go Dutch this time. John: No, I'll get it. I insist. Tanya: Okay, but next time it's on me. John: Let's see . . . The total without tax is $74.75. Tanya: Do you think we should leave 15 percent or more? John: The service was so-so. Our waiter was no great shakes. He seemed put out when we complained about our food. Tanya: What did he expect? The food left a lot to be desired! This is supposed to be such a great restaurant. I don't know what happened. John: I guess it's gone downhill. I'll leave 15 percent. I could leave less, but I don't want to be a cheapskate. Tanya: Yeah, we may want to come back here someday. John: Come back here? When hell freezes over!

idioms.meaning_en: to pay the bill for something, especially a meal at a restaurant
idioms.meaning_vi: trả tiền hóa đơn, thanh toán
idioms.example: My father always picks up the tab when we eat out as a family.
idioms.context: Let me pick up the tab.

idioms.meaning_en: used to tell someone that you will pay for them
idioms.meaning_vi: tôi mời, tôi sẽ trả tiền
idioms.example: Let's go for coffee; it's my treat.

idioms.meaning_en: to share the cost of a meal or an outing equally
idioms.meaning_vi: chia tiền, tiền ai nấy trả
idioms.example: We decided to go Dutch on our first date so neither of us felt awkward.

idioms.meaning_en: used to indicate that you will pay for the item or service
idioms.meaning_vi: để tôi trả tiền, tôi khao
idioms.example: Don't worry about the drinks; they are on me.
idioms.context: Okay, but next time it's on me.

idioms.meaning_en: neither very good nor very bad; mediocre
idioms.example: The concert was just so-so; I expected much more from them.

idioms.meaning_en: not particularly good, clever, or skillful
idioms.meaning_vi: không có gì đặc biệt, không giỏi lắm
idioms.example: He's a nice guy, but as a cook, he's no great shakes.
idioms.context: Our waiter was no great shakes.

idioms.meaning_vi: bực bội, khó chịu, phật lòng
idioms.example: She was clearly put out by his rude comments.
idioms.context: He seemed put out when we complained about our food.

idioms.idiom: left a lot to be desired
idioms.meaning_en: to be very unsatisfactory or lacking in quality
idioms.meaning_vi: còn nhiều thiếu sót, không tốt như mong đợi
idioms.example: The cleanliness of the hotel left a lot to be desired.
idioms.context: The food left a lot to be desired!

idioms.meaning_en: to decline in quality, health, or status
idioms.meaning_vi: xuống dốc, trở nên tồi tệ hơn
idioms.example: That bakery used to be great, but it has really gone downhill lately.

idioms.meaning_en: a person who is unwilling to spend money
idioms.example: He's such a cheapskate that he never leaves a tip.
idioms.context: I could leave less, but I don't want to be a cheapskate.

idioms.meaning_vi: không bao giờ, không đời nào
idioms.example: I'll go back to that store when hell freezes over!
idioms.context: Come back here? When hell freezes over!
